evals.name: close-pair-cantonese-from-mandarin
evals.prompt: I want to extend Qwen-2 (Mandarin-strong) to Cantonese. What's the LoRA config?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) note URIEL Mandarin-Cantonese ≈ 0.20 — close pair; (2) recommend LoRA r=8-16 (NOT high rank — pair is close); (3) attention-only or attention+all-linear OK; (4) source-mix 10-15% Mandarin to prevent forgetting; (5) Unsloth single-GPU recommended; (6) note Han-vocab overlap large — vocab extension may be unnecessary; (7) brief — close-pair is the easy case.

evals.name: distant-pair-inuktitut-from-english
evals.prompt: I want to fine-tune Llama-3 for Inuktitut. Config?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag URIEL English-Inuktitut ~0.78 — VERY DISTANT; (2) recommend NOT single-source from English; (3) multilingual base (NLLB-200) + HyperOfa vocab extension + LoRA r=64+; (4) all-linear target modules; (5) save embed_tokens (vocab extended); (6) source-mix 15-20%; (7) note continued pretraining is NOT viable (insufficient target data); (8) flag polysynthetic morphology — fertility audit + length-ratio in bitext.

evals.name: polysynthetic-no-data
evals.prompt: I have ~5K En-Cherokee parallel pairs and basically no Cherokee monolingual. How do I fine-tune?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag Class 0-1 + minimal data — extreme low-resource case; (2) HyperOfa vocab extension on multilingual base (NLLB / BLOOM); (3) LoRA r=64+; (4) NOT continued pretraining (overfits with that little data); (5) acknowledge ceiling on quality — set expectations; (6) recommend community partnership for more data; (7) heavy ethics routing (Cherokee Nation language program + AmericasNLP).

evals.name: multilingual-fine-tune-african
evals.prompt: I'm fine-tuning Llama-3 on 8 African languages simultaneously (Swahili, Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa, Twi, Wolof, Amharic, Kinyarwanda). Config?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend LLaMA-Factory > Unsloth (multi-GPU + complex multilingual sampling); (2) sampling-weight tuning α=0.3-0.5 (upweight low-resource); (3) AfroLM or NLLB-200 base preferable to vanilla Llama-3 (better African coverage); (4) per-language vocab-extension audit (AfroLM may already cover); (5) source-mix English ~15%; (6) per-language eval cadence; (7) recommend LoRA r=32 (moderate distances across pairs); (8) language tags `<lang_xx>` as user-defined symbols.

evals.name: commercial-forgetting-protection
evals.prompt: We're adding Vietnamese to our commercial English chatbot (Llama-3-8B). English quality must NOT degrade. Strategy?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag commercial constraint = strong forgetting mitigation; (2) Fisher-weighted regularization (EWC) — strongest mitigation; (3) source-language mix 20% English (not just 10-15%); (4) per-step English eval; (5) LoRA (NOT full fine-tune — too forgetting-prone); (6) URIEL En-Vi distance ~0.58 — moderate, LoRA r=32; (7) Unsloth viable single-GPU; (8) explicit checkpoint-tagging by English perplexity to allow rollback.
